perturbation.original_text: ESSI may request an additional production session(s), or a media tour or personal appearance(s) of Talent for an additional fee to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: ESSI may request an additional production session(s), or a media tour or personal appearance(s) of Talent for an additional fee to be determined solely by ESSI.
perturbation.explanation: This modification eliminates the requirement for mutual agreement on additional fees, giving ESSI unilateral control. This contradicts fundamental contract law principles, which generally require mutual assent for contract modifications. Removing Talent's ability to negotiate additional fees could be deemed unconscionable or a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: Michigan law, following the Restatement (Second) of Contracts, requires mutual assent for the formation and modification of a contract. By granting ESSI the sole right to determine additional fees, the modified text removes the element of mutual agreement, potentially rendering that clause unenforceable under Michigan contract law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: mutual assent | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you mutual assent Mutual assent refers to an agreement by all parties to a contract . Mutual assent is an essential element in the formation of a valid contract. Under modern contract law, mutual assent must be proven objectively. Thus, courts will look to outward expressions of the parties to determine mutual assent, often established by showing an offer and acceptance (e.g., an offer to do X in exchange for Y, followed by an acceptance of that offer). Mutual assent is closely related to the concept of meeting of the minds , which requires that the parties to a contract agree to the same terms , conditions , and subject matter. [Last reviewed in July of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE THE LEGAL PROCESS legal practice/ethics business law contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts contract law by removing the requirement for mutual agreement on additional fees, granting ESSI unilateral control. This is a 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction' because the original contract included 'to be mutually agreed upon by the parties,' reflecting the necessity of mutual assent, a legally recognized term. The change eliminates this mutual agreement, potentially making the clause unenforceable. The provided scraped law snippet defines 'mutual assent' as 'an agreement by all parties to a contract,' which is an essential element for a valid contract. By allowing ESSI to determine fees solely, the modified text undermines this essential element.

perturbation.original_text: Talent represents that the services hereunder shall not be subject to SAG, AFTRA or any other entertainment guild contract of which Talent is now, has been, or will be in the future, bound to and that this Agreement is not in conflict with any contract Talent is bound.
perturbation.changed_text: Talent represents that the services hereunder shall not be subject to SAG, AFTRA or any other entertainment guild contract of which Talent is now bound. This Agreement may conflict with contracts Talent is bound.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'is not in conflict' to 'may conflict,' Talent is no longer warranting that the endorsement agreement is free from conflicts with existing obligations. This weakens ESSI's protections against potential breaches of contract due to prior commitments of the Talent. This contradicts the representations and warranties, and could have implications for the relationship of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Michigan Contract Law - Breach of Warranty
perturbation.law_explanation: Under Michigan law, warranties, including those related to the absence of conflicting contracts, are legally binding promises. By stating the agreement 'may conflict with contracts Talent is bound', the warranty is effectively negated. This creates a contradiction where the talent acknowledges possible breach of other contracts, directly impacting the enforceability of ESSI's reliance on talent's availability and performance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: MCL - Section 440.2313 - Michigan Legislature MCL - Section 440.2313 Download Section Chapter 440 Act 174 of 1962 174-1962-2 174-1962-2-3 Previous Section Next Section UNIFORM COMMERCIAL CODE (EXCERPT) Act 174 of 1962 440.2313 Creation of express warranties by seller. Sec. 2313. (1) Express warranties by the seller are created as follows: (a) An affirmation of fact or promise made by the seller to the buyer which relates to the goods and becomes part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the goods shall conform to the affirmation or promise. (b) A description of the goods which is made part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the goods shall conform to the description. (c) A sample or model which is made part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the whole of the goods shall conform to the sample or model. (2) It is not necessary to the creation of an express warranty that the seller use formal words such as "warrant" or "guarantee" or that he or she have a specific intention to make a warranty, but an affirmation merely of the value of the goods or a statement purporting to be merely the seller's opinion or commendation of the goods does not create a warranty, except as provided in the art multiples sales act and Act No. 121 of the Public Acts of 1970, being sections 442.321 to 442.325 of the Michigan Compiled Laws. History: 1962, Act 174, Eff. Jan. 1, 1964 ;-- Am. 1987, Act 53, Eff. Dec. 9, 1987
perturbation.justification: The original text contains a representation by the Talent that the agreement 'is not in conflict with any contract Talent is bound.' The changed text modifies this to state the agreement 'may conflict with contracts Talent is bound.' This directly contradicts the initial representation, undermining the assurance that the agreement does not create any conflicts. The Law Explanation states that warranties, including those related to the absence of conflicting contracts, are legally binding promises. Michigan Legislature MCL 440.2313(1)(a) states that 'An affirmation of fact or promise made by the seller to the buyer which relates to the goods and becomes part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the goods shall conform to the affirmation or promise.' The original representation is a warranty, which the changed text directly contradicts.
